blocks.content: After purifying Mercede into pure magic, Marguerite opened the storage room door.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: Come out.
Rian emerged from the unpurified body, one eye closed.
He didn't know the details of their conversation, but he could sense from the Duke's eyes that someone had died.
As Rian exited the storage room, Marguerite busily gathered experimental tools.
This was originally Mercede's task.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: We don't have much time. I had intended to study you in depth, but we must begin the modifications immediately.
Lethe would return.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Why are you doing this?

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: …What do you mean?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: The succubus is dead, and Lethe has left. She must have been important to you.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: That's precisely why I'm saying this. When the president returns, it won't be like this. This is the final warning. It's the last favor she's granting us.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: That's what I want to say.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Rian felt uneasy.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: If Lethe is like Satan, she wouldn't leave over something like this. She's being sincere. You know this, so why are you helping us?
Hmm.
Marguerite paused.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: Lady Lethe is a good person. She's cold and fair, but she's also kind. That doesn't fit well with hell. But you mustn't forget, Lady Lethe is not a demon.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: She's the manager of this world. She's fundamentally different from us, even from Satan.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: But that doesn't change her feelings.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Feelings?
Marguerite chuckled.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: Then tell me. I honestly don't know. When Mercede died, Lady Lethe stepped back despite the perilous situation for hell. Was that feelings? Or…
A terrible truth.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Is she programmed that way?

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: The president said she sent the chief secretary Monoras to discuss your error. But he didn't come, and Yahweh arrived first. Do you think that's luck? I don't. Because Monoras is also a manager.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: What do you mean…

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: How can a human who lives only a few decades understand the grand order? But your master might have some suspicions. That's the level of mental ability. What I'm saying is, where does reality end and illusion begin?
Marguerite checked Rian's scan information.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: Your abilities are based on the master identity code of this world. The chains of Gehenna broke because that authority transcended the system, causing an error. So, are you from the outside world?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Rian couldn't answer.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: We don't know. That's the limit. Even if the outside world exists, we won't know who we are unless we directly connect with its information. You might be a program too.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I decide and act on my own will.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: I know. But what if even that is programmed? Understand? We can't conclude this way. But the manager knows who they are. You can define them as you wish, but remember this.
The chains of Gehenna pulled from Rian's chest were held in the Duke's grasp.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: It might be different from what we imagine. That's the 0.5-dimensional wall. If you truly want to protect Yahweh, trust no one.
As Rian remained silent, Marguerite examined the broken part of the chain.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: Let's begin.
Stang National Magic School
Shirone had promised to allow parents to observe Wizard's training under the condition that he would train her.
But perhaps it would have been better if he hadn't made that promise.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: Oh my…
With all the teachers and students watching, Wizard was being bombarded by Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Your reactions are too slow!
The seven-year-old child was repeatedly pushed back by powerful magic that could devastate the area.
Her parents, pale with fear, screamed.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: Wizard!
As the Photon Cannon exploded, a loud noise echoed, and debris scattered across the wide training ground.
Hoo!
Through the smoke, Wizard could be seen casting a defensive spell and exhaling deeply.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's over.
Everyone thought so, but then Wizard's eyes lit up, and a barrier formed.
Boom!
The air curtain rippled visibly, and Wizard flew through the air and crashed to the ground.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: Wi-Wizard?
After a long time of no movement, Wizard stood up, her broken right arm dangling.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Haa. Haa.
She muttered softly.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I blocked it.
Shirone took another step.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She broke her limit. Just once more…
At that moment, her mother broke free from the teachers and ran over.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: You bastard!
As Shirone turned, her hands grabbed his collar.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: What are you doing to my daughter! Her arm is broken! She's only seven!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You promised to let me handle it. If it's uncomfortable to watch, you don't have to be here.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: Is that why you made that promise? To show this? You misjudged me. I don't need money or anything. End this now.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I can heal her arm. If I cast a spell…
A slap echoed.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: Oh my…
As the mother, who had slapped Shirone, fumed, the teachers' faces turned pale.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: She's crazy. She hit one of the Five Great Stars.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone had deliberately offered his cheek.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah.
The mother, coming to her senses, looked at her hand.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: It wasn't because she had hit one of the Five Great Stars, but because she had never hit anyone before.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: My mom would have done the same.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: Mother.
Shirone turned back to her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wizard is an exceptional child. Most seven-year-olds would cry if their arm broke. They wouldn't endure it. That means she already understands pain with her mind.

blocks.speaker: Father
blocks.content: What do you mean, understanding pain? Wizard isn't crazy. She's too scared to even cry.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's quite the opposite.
Shirone's voice rose slightly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's because she's exceptional. She can do it, so I'm making her. The things Wizard is learning now are knowledge imbued with the blood and sweat of countless talents she could never achieve alone. Because she has benefited from it, in a dangerous situation for humanity, she too…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Must sacrifice herself.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: …I'm sorry.
Bowing his head in apology to the parents, Shirone turned and left the training ground.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: That's it for today.
As no one saw him off, Wizard, who had finished her treatment, approached.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Mom, Dad, what's going on?

blocks.speaker: Father
blocks.content: Wizard!

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: Did it hurt a lot? How hard was it?

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Where's brother? Is the training over?

blocks.speaker: Father
blocks.content: Yes, it's over. I told him to stop. I'll never leave you with someone like that again.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Hmm.
Wizard stared at Shirone's retreating figure.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Thanks, Mom. I almost died this time. But I'm really sorry…

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Please don't interfere with my training from now on.
Her parents' faces went blank.

blocks.speaker: Father
blocks.content: Wi-Wizard? What do you mean…

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Sorry, I need to go see my brother. I have some questions about today's training.
In a hurry, Wizard broke away from the crowd and followed Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: It's talent that draws her.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: The only adult Wizard acknowledged.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: Instinctively, she knew that following Shirone closely was the only way to survive.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: There's no doubt about her potential, but I was uncertain about her disposition.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: When Shirone said she might die, she smiled brightly and agreed.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: The time for hesitation was short.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: In the mind of a genius, all the tasks she had to handle must have flashed by.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: I understand the feelings of the Five Great Stars.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Brother!
Wizard's cheerful voice came from outside the training ground.
